repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure_v1: sharp finite ADE bounds from shared local-network completions; not estimator/covariance equivalence

published_prior_art.cite: Leung2022ANI + Leung2023NCRI + BasseDingFellerToulis2024; oracle equality is a sanity benchmark, not a new kernel
closest: Leung2022ANI, Leung2023NCRI, BasseDingFellerToulis2024, GaoDing2025; none gives a joint ANI/NCR/RT iff frontier
closest: BasseDingFellerToulis2024 invalid design permutations; not an open-class ANI/NCR/RT separation

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Misses GaoDing2025, a close Journal of Econometrics paper on regression-based point estimators and network-HAC covariance for network experiments; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship until the frontier is made computable from observables and positioned against that result.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The proposal advertises an observable/checkable frontier, but mu_perp is defined using U_M,n = W_rho,n^{1/2}(I-Pi_Mr)T_n, an omitted true potential-outcome tail, so the flagship diagnostic is not yet observable as claimed.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The phrase 'efficiency loss is determined by V_NCR(b)-V_RT(Omega)' needs a scalarization or PSD order; a raw covariance-operator difference need not define a loss.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace mu_perp with an actually observable residual-score distance, or explicitly recast it as a sensitivity parameter and add a separate observable lower/upper diagnostic.
Add GaoDing2025 to Related work and explain why their WLS/Hajek/network-HAC design-based theory does not already settle the ANI point-score and HAC part of the frontier.
Define the strict-separation covariance gap through a quadratic form in the target contrast, trace on the active score cone, Loewner order with conditions, or an efficiency-bound scalar.

recommended_next_step: Revise the flagship frontier so the threshold is observable or explicitly sensitivity-based, then add the Gao-Ding comparison and a scalar covariance-loss definition.
